message:
Add shellcheck disable for checkpoint function string parameters

Suppress SC2178/SC2128 warnings for checkpoint_review_state() which
intentionally uses space-separated strings (not arrays) for the
completed_repos and pending_repos parameters.

Part of bd-z89z implementation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-ff8h","title":"Implement GraphQL batched repository discovery","description":"# Task: Implement GraphQL Batched Repository Discovery

## Purpose
Efficiently discover open issues and PRs across all configured repos using GraphQL alias batching, reducing API calls from O(n) to O(n/25).

## Background: Why GraphQL Batching?
- REST API: 1 call per repo for issues + 1 for PRs = 2n calls
- GraphQL aliases: Query 25 repos in single call = n/25 calls
- For 100 repos: 200 REST calls → 4 GraphQL calls (50× reduction)
- Avoids rate limiting, faster discovery

## Implementation Details

### gh_api_graphql_repo_batch()
```bash
gh_api_graphql_repo_batch() {
    local chunk="$1"
    local q="query {"
    local i=0

    while IFS= read -r repo_id; do
        [[ -z "$repo_id" ]] && continue
        local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
        local name="${repo_id#*/}"

        q+=" repo${i}: repository(owner:\"${owner}\", name:\"${name}\") {"
        q+=" nameWithOwner isArchived isFork updatedAt"
        # Issues with metadata for scoring
        q+=" issues(states:OPEN, first:50, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt"
        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
        q+="   }"
        q+=" }"
        # PRs with metadata for scoring
        q+=" pullRequests(states:OPEN, first:20, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt isDraft"
        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
        q+="   }"
        q+=" }"
        # Oldest open issue for staleness
        q+=" oldestIssue: issues(states:OPEN, first:1, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:ASC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { createdAt }"
        q+=" }"
        q+=" }"
        ((i++))
    done <<< "$chunk"

    q+=" }"
    
    gh api graphql -f query="$q" 2>/dev/null
}
```

### parse_graphql_work_items()
Uses jq to flatten response into TSV for easy Bash parsing:
```bash
parse_graphql_work_items() {
    local resp="$1"

    echo "$resp" | jq -r '
        .data | to_entries[] | select(.value != null) |
        select(.value.isArchived != true) |
        select(.value.isFork != true) |
        .value as $repo |
        (
            # Issues
            ($repo.issues.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "issue", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
             .createdAt, .updatedAt, "false"] | @tsv
        ),
        (
            # PRs
            ($repo.pullRequests.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "pr", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
             .createdAt, .updatedAt, (.isDraft | tostring)] | @tsv
        )
    ' 2>/dev/null
}
```

### chunk_repo_ids()
Split repo list into chunks of 25:
```bash
chunk_repo_ids() {
    local chunk_size="$1"
    shift
    local repos=("$@")
    local count=0
    local chunk=""
    
    for repo in "${repos[@]}"; do
        chunk+="$repo"$'\n'
        ((count++))
        if [[ $count -ge $chunk_size ]]; then
            echo "$chunk"
            chunk=""
            count=0
        fi
    done
    
    [[ -n "$chunk" ]] && echo "$chunk"
}
```

### discover_work_items()
Main discovery function that:
1. Gets all repos from config
2. Resolves to owner/repo format
3. Filters non-GitHub hosts
4. Chunks into batches of 25
5. Executes GraphQL queries
6. Parses responses into work items
7. Calculates priority scores
8. Sorts by score descending
9. Applies max_repos limit if specified

## Data Format
Work item string format (pipe-separated):
```
repo_id|type|number|title|score|level|created_at|updated_at
owner/repo|issue|42|Authentication failing|85|HIGH|2024-12-15T...|2025-01-01T...
```

## Error Handling
- Skip repos that fail resolution
- Continue on individual query failures
- Log warnings for rate limit responses
- Graceful degradation if GraphQL unavailable

## Dependencies
- jq for JSON parsing (already assumed by ru)
- gh CLI with GraphQL support

## Testing
- Mock GraphQL response with fixture
- Verify chunking works correctly
- Verify archived/forked repos filtered
- Verify TSV parsing handles edge cases
- Verify 25-repo chunks respected

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single API call for up to 25 repos
- [ ] Issues and PRs both discovered
- [ ] Labels extracted for scoring
- [ ] Archived/forked repos excluded
- [ ] Handles 100+ repos efficiently","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.462046988-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.462046988-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ff8h","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.851431049-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- [ ] Archived/forked repos excluded
- [ ] Handles 100+ repos efficiently","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.462046988-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:45:11.008373378-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ff8h","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.851431049-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-fi65","title":"Implement basic mode TUI (gum-based fallback)","description":"Task: Implement Basic Mode TUI (gum-based)

Purpose
-------
Provide a simple but functional TUI when advanced dashboard not available,
using gum for styled output and selection.

When Used
---------
- gum available but not full TUI mode
- User prefers simpler interface
- Debugging/development

Implementation
--------------

show_question_basic_mode()
  Display single question with gum styling:
  
  gum style --border rounded --padding "1 2" \
    --border-foreground "#fab387" \
    "Question from: $repo"
  
  echo "$question"
  
  Build options array from JSON
  gum choose "${option_labels[@]}"

show_discovery_summary_basic()
  gum style --border rounded "Discovery Summary"
  echo "Repos with activity: $count"
  echo "Total work items: $items"
  echo "High priority: $high"

show_session_status_basic()
  Simple text status:
  for session in sessions:
    echo "$repo: $state"

basic_mode_loop()
  Sequential question processing:
  while questions pending:
    show_question_basic_mode
    answer = gum choose
    send_answer
    mark_answered

gum_confirm_wrapper()
  with fallback to read prompt:
  
  if GUM_AVAILABLE:
    gum confirm "$prompt"
  else:
    read -rp "$prompt [Y/n] " yn
    case "${yn,,}" in y|yes) return 0 ;; esac

Progress Display
----------------
gum spin --title "Processing..." -- command
gum style --foreground "green" "✓ Done"

Formatting Helpers
------------------
format_priority_badge()
  case priority:
    CRITICAL: gum style --foreground "red" "CRITICAL"
    HIGH: gum style --foreground "orange" "HIGH"
    NORMAL: gum style --foreground "yellow" "NORMAL"
    LOW: gum style --foreground "gray" "LOW"

ANSI Fallback
-------------
If gum not available, use ANSI codes:
- Bold: \033[1m
- Colors: \033[31m (red), etc.
- Box drawing: Unicode ─│┌┐└┘

Testing
-------
- Verify gum styling renders
- Verify choice selection works
- Verify ANSI fallback works
- Test without gum installed

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Questions display with styling
- [ ] Options selectable via gum choose
- [ ] Progress shown with gum spin
- [ ] Works without gum (ANSI fallback)
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:40.686170236-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:40.686170236-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fi65","depends_on_id":"bd-4ps0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:13.195307516-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -209,6 +209,6 @@
 {"id":"bd-xdrt","title":"Phase 3: ntm Integration (Robot Mode, Activity Detection)","description":"Phase 3: ntm Integration

Overview
--------
Integrate with ntm (Named Tmux Manager) robot mode for advanced orchestration
capabilities: activity detection, health monitoring, and question routing.

Why ntm?
--------
ntm provides capabilities beyond basic tmux:
- Velocity-based activity detection (chars/sec)
- Health monitoring with error pattern detection
- Robot mode JSON API for programmatic control
- Workflow pipelines for complex multi-step tasks
- Session management and routing

Components
----------

3.1 ntm Driver Implementation
   Implement the unified driver interface using ntm robot mode API:
   - ntm --robot-spawn: Create sessions
   - ntm --robot-status: Query session state
   - ntm --robot-health: Monitor session health
   - ntm --robot-route: Get best pane for new work
   - ntm --robot-send: Deliver messages to sessions

3.2 Activity State Detection
   ntm detects Claude state via output velocity:
   - GENERATING: velocity > 10 chars/sec (active output)
   - WAITING: velocity < 1 chars/sec + prompt pattern
   - THINKING: low velocity, no prompt
   - STALLED: velocity == 0 for > 30 seconds
   - ERROR: error patterns detected

3.3 Wait Reason Extraction
   When WAITING, determine WHY:
   - ask_user_question: AskUserQuestion tool detected
   - agent_question_text: Question in plain text
   - external_prompt: Git/SSH/auth prompt
   - unknown: At prompt, reason unclear

3.4 Health Monitoring
   Monitor session health continuously:
   - Detect rate limits (429, quota exceeded)
   - Detect crashes (panic, SIGSEGV)
   - Detect stalls (no output for extended period)
   - Trigger recovery actions

3.5 Workflow Pipeline
   Define github-review.yaml workflow:
   - verify_prerequisites: Check gh auth
   - understand_codebase: Create/update digest
   - review_issues_prs: Main review work
   - finalize_artifacts: Validate plan created

3.6 Rate-Limit Governor
   Adaptive concurrency based on real limits:
   - Query GitHub API for remaining quota
   - Detect model rate limits from 429s
   - Adjust parallelism dynamically
   - Circuit breaker for cascading failures

Exit Criteria
-------------
- ntm driver passes all interface tests
- Activity detection works reliably
- Wait reasons extracted correctly
- Health monitoring catches common issues
- Rate-limit governor adjusts concurrency

Estimated Effort
----------------
~400 lines Bash + ~300 lines in ntm (Go)
Note: ntm changes are separate project","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:42.232591922-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:42.232591922-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdrt","depends_on_id":"bd-5yy3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:26.666656889-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-z89z","title":"Implement atomic state persistence with flock","description":"# Task: Implement Atomic State Persistence

## Purpose
Store review state (outcomes, questions, runs) in JSON files with atomic writes and flock-based locking to prevent corruption from concurrent access or interrupted writes.

## Background: Why Atomic Writes?
- Ctrl+C during write = corrupted JSON
- Concurrent reads during write = partial data
- Power failure = lost state
- Solution: write to temp file, then atomic mv

## State Files

### 1. review-state.json
```json
{
  "version": 2,
  "repos": {
    "owner/repo": {
      "last_review": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "last_review_run_id": "abc123",
      "issues_reviewed": 3,
      "prs_reviewed": 1,
      "issues_resolved": 2,
      "outcome": "completed",
      "duration_seconds": 847,
      "digest_hash": "sha256:..."
    }
  },
  "items": {
    "owner/repo#issue-42": {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "last_review": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "outcome": "fixed",
      "notes": "Path handling fixed for Windows"
    }
  },
  "runs": {
    "abc123": {
      "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:00:00Z",
      "completed_at": "2025-01-04T11:30:00Z",
      "repos_processed": 8,
      "items_processed": 14,
      "questions_asked": 12,
      "mode": "ntm"
    }
  }
}
```

### 2. review-questions.json
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q_abc123",
      "run_id": "run_xyz",
      "repo": "owner/repo",
      "session_id": "ru-review-owner-repo",
      "pane_id": "1",
      "context": "Should I refactor...",
      "options": ["a) Minimal fix", "b) Full refactor"],
      "priority": "normal",
      "detected_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Implementation

### State Lock
```bash
# Global state lock (separate from review session lock)
STATE_LOCK_FD=201

acquire_state_lock() {
    local lock_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/state.lock"
    exec 201>"$lock_file"
    flock -x 201
}

release_state_lock() {
    flock -u 201 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Scoped lock helper
with_state_lock() {
    acquire_state_lock
    "$@"
    local rc=$?
    release_state_lock
    return $rc
}
```

### Atomic JSON Write
```bash
write_json_atomic() {
    local file="$1"
    local content="$2"
    local tmp_file="${file}.tmp.$$"
    
    # Write to temp file
    echo "$content" > "$tmp_file"
    
    # Validate JSON before committing
    if ! jq empty "$tmp_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        rm -f "$tmp_file"
        log_error "Invalid JSON, refusing to write: $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Atomic move
    mv "$tmp_file" "$file"
}
```

### Read with Lock
```bash
read_state_json() {
    local file="$1"
    local default="${2:-{}}"
    
    acquire_state_lock
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        cat "$file"
    else
        echo "$default"
    fi
    release_state_lock
}
```

### Update State
```bash
update_review_state() {
    local updates="$1"  # jq filter
    local state_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review-state.json"
    
    acquire_state_lock
    
    local current
    if [[ -f "$state_file" ]]; then
        current=$(cat "$state_file")
    else
        current='{"version":2,"repos":{},"items":{},"runs":{}}'
    fi
    
    local updated
    updated=$(echo "$current" | jq "$updates")
    
    write_json_atomic "$state_file" "$updated"
    
    release_state_lock
}
```

### Record Item Outcome
```bash
record_item_outcome() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local item_type="$2"
    local number="$3"
    local outcome="$4"
    local notes="$5"
    
    local item_key="${repo_id}#${item_type}-${number}"
    local now
    now=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    
    update_review_state "
        .items[\"$item_key\"] = {
            \"type\": \"$item_type\",
            \"number\": $number,
            \"last_review\": \"$now\",
            \"outcome\": \"$outcome\",
            \"notes\": $(echo "$notes" | jq -R .)
        }
    "
}
```

## Checkpoint State (for Resume)
```bash
checkpoint_review_state() {
    local checkpoint_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review-checkpoint.json"
    
    local state
    state=$(cat << EOF
{
  "version": 1,
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "run_id": "$REVIEW_RUN_ID",
  "mode": "$REVIEW_MODE",
  "repos_total": ${#REVIEW_REPOS[@]},
  "repos_completed": ${#COMPLETED_REPOS[@]},
  "repos_pending": $(printf '%s\n' "${PENDING_REPOS[@]}" | jq -R . | jq -s .),
  "questions_pending": $(cat "$RU_STATE_DIR/review-questions.json" 2>/dev/null || echo '{"questions":[]}')
}
EOF
)
    
    with_state_lock write_json_atomic "$checkpoint_file" "$state"
}
```

## Cleanup Old State
```bash
cleanup_old_review_state() {
    local max_age_days="${1:-30}"
    local state_dir="$RU_STATE_DIR"
    
    # Clean old worktrees
    find "$state_dir/worktrees" -maxdepth 1 -type d -mtime "+$max_age_days" \
        -exec rm -rf {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Prune old runs from state file
    local cutoff
    cutoff=$(date -u -d "$max_age_days days ago" +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ 2>/dev/null || \
             date -u -v-${max_age_days}d +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    
    update_review_state "
        .runs |= with_entries(select(.value.started_at > \"$cutoff\"))
    "
}
```

## Testing
- Write, crash, verify temp file cleaned up
- Concurrent writes don't corrupt
- Read during write gets consistent data
- Checkpoint and resume works
- Malformed JSON rejected

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All state writes are atomic (temp + mv)
- [ ] flock prevents concurrent write corruption
- [ ] JSON validated before commit
- [ ] Checkpoint captures full state

+{"id":"bd-z89z","title":"Implement atomic state persistence with flock","description":"# Task: Implement Atomic State Persistence

## Purpose
Store review state (outcomes, questions, runs) in JSON files with atomic writes and flock-based locking to prevent corruption from concurrent access or interrupted writes.

## Background: Why Atomic Writes?
- Ctrl+C during write = corrupted JSON
- Concurrent reads during write = partial data
- Power failure = lost state
- Solution: write to temp file, then atomic mv

## State Files

### 1. review-state.json
```json
{
  "version": 2,
  "repos": {
    "owner/repo": {
      "last_review": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "last_review_run_id": "abc123",
      "issues_reviewed": 3,
      "prs_reviewed": 1,
      "issues_resolved": 2,
      "outcome": "completed",
      "duration_seconds": 847,
      "digest_hash": "sha256:..."
    }
  },
  "items": {
    "owner/repo#issue-42": {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "last_review": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "outcome": "fixed",
      "notes": "Path handling fixed for Windows"
    }
  },
  "runs": {
    "abc123": {
      "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:00:00Z",
      "completed_at": "2025-01-04T11:30:00Z",
      "repos_processed": 8,
      "items_processed": 14,
      "questions_asked": 12,
      "mode": "ntm"
    }
  }
}
```

### 2. review-questions.json
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q_abc123",
      "run_id": "run_xyz",
      "repo": "owner/repo",
      "session_id": "ru-review-owner-repo",
      "pane_id": "1",
      "context": "Should I refactor...",
      "options": ["a) Minimal fix", "b) Full refactor"],
      "priority": "normal",
      "detected_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Implementation

### State Lock
```bash
# Global state lock (separate from review session lock)
STATE_LOCK_FD=201

acquire_state_lock() {
    local lock_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/state.lock"
    exec 201>"$lock_file"
    flock -x 201
}

release_state_lock() {
    flock -u 201 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Scoped lock helper
with_state_lock() {
    acquire_state_lock
    "$@"
    local rc=$?
    release_state_lock
    return $rc
}
```

### Atomic JSON Write
```bash
write_json_atomic() {
    local file="$1"
    local content="$2"
    local tmp_file="${file}.tmp.$$"
    
    # Write to temp file
    echo "$content" > "$tmp_file"
    
    # Validate JSON before committing
    if ! jq empty "$tmp_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        rm -f "$tmp_file"
        log_error "Invalid JSON, refusing to write: $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Atomic move
    mv "$tmp_file" "$file"
}
```

### Read with Lock
```bash
read_state_json() {
    local file="$1"
    local default="${2:-{}}"
    
    acquire_state_lock
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        cat "$file"
    else
        echo "$default"
    fi
    release_state_lock
}
```

### Update State
```bash
update_review_state() {
    local updates="$1"  # jq filter
    local state_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review-state.json"
    
    acquire_state_lock
    
    local current
    if [[ -f "$state_file" ]]; then
        current=$(cat "$state_file")
    else
        current='{"version":2,"repos":{},"items":{},"runs":{}}'
    fi
    
    local updated
    updated=$(echo "$current" | jq "$updates")
    
    write_json_atomic "$state_file" "$updated"
    
    release_state_lock
}
```

### Record Item Outcome
```bash
record_item_outcome() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local item_type="$2"
    local number="$3"
    local outcome="$4"
    local notes="$5"
    
    local item_key="${repo_id}#${item_type}-${number}"
    local now
    now=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    
    update_review_state "
        .items[\"$item_key\"] = {
            \"type\": \"$item_type\",
            \"number\": $number,
            \"last_review\": \"$now\",
            \"outcome\": \"$outcome\",
            \"notes\": $(echo "$notes" | jq -R .)
        }
    "
}
```

## Checkpoint State (for Resume)
```bash
checkpoint_review_state() {
    local checkpoint_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review-checkpoint.json"
    
    local state
    state=$(cat << EOF
{
  "version": 1,
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "run_id": "$REVIEW_RUN_ID",
  "mode": "$REVIEW_MODE",
  "repos_total": ${#REVIEW_REPOS[@]},
  "repos_completed": ${#COMPLETED_REPOS[@]},
  "repos_pending": $(printf '%s\n' "${PENDING_REPOS[@]}" | jq -R . | jq -s .),
  "questions_pending": $(cat "$RU_STATE_DIR/review-questions.json" 2>/dev/null || echo '{"questions":[]}')
}
EOF
)
    
    with_state_lock write_json_atomic "$checkpoint_file" "$state"
}
```

## Cleanup Old State
```bash
cleanup_old_review_state() {
    local max_age_days="${1:-30}"
    local state_dir="$RU_STATE_DIR"
    
    # Clean old worktrees
    find "$state_dir/worktrees" -maxdepth 1 -type d -mtime "+$max_age_days" \
        -exec rm -rf {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Prune old runs from state file
    local cutoff
    cutoff=$(date -u -d "$max_age_days days ago" +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ 2>/dev/null || \
             date -u -v-${max_age_days}d +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    
    update_review_state "
        .runs |= with_entries(select(.value.started_at > \"$cutoff\"))
    "
}
```

## Testing
- Write, crash, verify temp file cleaned up
- Concurrent writes don't corrupt
- Read during write gets consistent data
- Checkpoint and resume works
- Malformed JSON rejected

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All state writes are atomic (temp + mv)
- [ ] flock prevents concurrent write corruption
- [ ] JSON validated before commit
- [ ] Checkpoint captures full state
- [ ] Old state cleaned up automatically","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:18:52.733473052-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:45:19.174289659-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:45:19.174289659-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all state persistence functions with flock-based locking and atomic JSON writes.  {"id":"bd-zki","title":"Test task","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:08:45.034918173-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:15:28.281051598-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:15:28.281051598-05:00","close_reason":"Test issue - removing"}
 {"id":"bd-zlws","title":"Implement git worktree preparation for isolated reviews","description":"# Task: Implement Git Worktree Preparation

## Purpose
Create isolated git worktrees for each repo being reviewed, so AI agents can make changes without affecting the main working directory.

## Background: Why Worktrees?
- Main repo stays untouched until explicit apply
- Easy rollback: just delete the worktree
- Parallel reviews don't conflict
- Clear separation of concerns
- No risk of polluting uncommitted work

## Implementation Details

### prepare_review_worktrees()
```bash
prepare_review_worktrees() {
    local repos=("$@")
    local base="$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees/$REVIEW_RUN_ID"
    mkdir -p "$base"

    for repo_info in "${repos[@]}"; do
        local repo_spec issues prs updated_at oldest
        IFS='|' read -r repo_spec issues prs updated_at oldest <<< "$repo_info"

        local url branch custom_name local_path repo_id
        resolve_repo_spec "$repo_spec" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT" \
            url branch custom_name local_path repo_id

        # CRITICAL: Refuse to run on dirty trees
        ensure_clean_or_fail "$local_path"

        # Create worktree path (sanitize repo_id for filesystem)
        local wt_path="$base/${repo_id//\//_}"
        local wt_branch="ru/review/$REVIEW_RUN_ID/${repo_id//\//-}"

        # Fetch latest from remote
        git -C "$local_path" fetch --quiet 2>/dev/null || true

        # Respect branch pins from repo spec
        local base_ref="${branch:-HEAD}"
        
        # Create worktree with new branch
        git -C "$local_path" worktree add -b "$wt_branch" "$wt_path" "$base_ref" >/dev/null

        # Create .ru directory for artifacts
        mkdir -p "$wt_path/.ru"

        # Record mapping for later phases
        record_worktree_mapping "$repo_id" "$wt_path" "$wt_branch"
        
        log_verbose "Created worktree: $repo_id → $wt_path"
    done
}
```

### ensure_clean_or_fail()
```bash
ensure_clean_or_fail() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    
    if ! is_git_repo "$repo_path"; then
        log_error "$repo_path is not a git repository"
        return 1
    fi
    
    local status
    status=$(git -C "$repo_path" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [[ -n "$status" ]]; then
        log_error "Repository has uncommitted changes: $repo_path"
        log_error "Please commit or stash changes before running review"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}
```

### record_worktree_mapping()
```bash
record_worktree_mapping() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local wt_path="$2"
    local wt_branch="$3"
    
    local mapping_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees/$REVIEW_RUN_ID/mapping.json"
    
    # Initialize if doesn't exist
    [[ ! -f "$mapping_file" ]] && echo '{}' > "$mapping_file"
    
    # Add mapping atomically
    local tmp_file="${mapping_file}.tmp.$$"
    jq --arg repo "$repo_id" \
       --arg path "$wt_path" \
       --arg branch "$wt_branch" \
       '.[$repo] = {"path": $path, "branch": $branch}' \
       "$mapping_file" > "$tmp_file"
    mv "$tmp_file" "$mapping_file"
}
```

### get_worktree_mapping()
```bash
get_worktree_mapping() {
    local repo_info="$1"
    local -n _repo_id=$2
    local -n _wt_path=$3
    
    # Extract repo_id from repo_info
    _repo_id="${repo_info%%|*}"
    
    local mapping_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees/$REVIEW_RUN_ID/mapping.json"
    _wt_path=$(jq -r --arg repo "$_repo_id" '.[$repo].path // ""' "$mapping_file")
}
```

### cleanup_review_worktrees()
```bash
cleanup_review_worktrees() {
    local run_id="${1:-$REVIEW_RUN_ID}"
    local base="$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees/$run_id"
    
    [[ ! -d "$base" ]] && return 0
    
    local mapping_file="$base/mapping.json"
    
    if [[ -f "$mapping_file" ]]; then
        # Remove each worktree properly
        while IFS= read -r repo_id; do
            local wt_path
            wt_path=$(jq -r --arg repo "$repo_id" '.[$repo].path' "$mapping_file")
            
            if [[ -d "$wt_path" ]]; then
                # Find the main repo and remove worktree
                local main_repo
                main_repo=$(dirname "$(dirname "$wt_path")")  # heuristic
                git -C "$main_repo" worktree remove --force "$wt_path" 2>/dev/null || \
                    rm -rf "$wt_path"
            fi
        done < <(jq -r 'keys[]' "$mapping_file")
    fi
    
    rm -rf "$base"
}
```

## Directory Structure
```
~/.local/state/ru/worktrees/
└── 20250104-103000-12345/          # Run ID
    ├── mapping.json                 # Repo → worktree mapping
    ├── owner_repo1/                 # Worktree for owner/repo1
    │   ├── .git                     # Worktree git link
    │   ├── .ru/                     # Artifacts directory
    │   │   ├── review-plan.json     # Plan artifact
    │   │   ├── repo-digest.md       # Cached understanding
    │   │   └── session.log          # Session transcript
    │   └── [repo files]
    └── owner_repo2/
        └── ...
```

## Branch Naming Convention
```
ru/review/{RUN_ID}/{repo-id}
ru/review/20250104-103000-12345/owner-repo
```

## Edge Cases
- Repo doesn't exist locally: Skip with warning (or auto-clone?)
- Repo is not a git repo: Error and skip
- Worktree already exists: Error (shouldn't happen with unique run IDs)
- Disk full: Proper error message

## Testing
- Create worktree, verify isolation
- Make changes in worktree, verify main is unchanged
- Cleanup removes worktrees properly
- Branch naming is correct
- Mapping file is accurate

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Worktrees created in correct location
- [ ] Dirty repos rejected with clear message
- [ ] Branch pins from repo spec respected
- [ ] .ru directory created for artifacts
- [ ] Mapping file tracks all worktrees
- [ ] Cleanup removes worktrees and branches","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:18:52.480379897-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:18:52.480379897-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zlws","depends_on_id":"bd-5jph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.892506308-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
